course_description.overview: Amateur multi-stage road cycling event mirroring the professional Tour de Suisse, covering approximately 1015 km across Switzerland with mountainous and rolling terrain over several days.

key_segments.name: Stage 1
key_segments.description: Start in Einsiedeln, following pro race routes.

key_segments.name: Mountain Stages
key_segments.description: High Alps climbs similar to pro Queen Stage.

key_segments.name: Final Stages
key_segments.description: Rolling terrain leading to overall finish.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: High difficulty due to total distance, significant elevation in Swiss Alps, and multi-day format testing endurance on paved mountain roads akin to UCI WorldTour stages.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Zurich Airport (ZRH), then train or drive to start in Einsiedeln or host regions; aligns with pro race host cities announced autumn 2025.
logistics.accommodation: Book hotels in daily host towns matching pro Tour de Suisse regions; camping or bike-friendly stays in Swiss Alps areas.
logistics.registration_details: Opens TBD via official site; entry fee TBD, limited field as amateur companion event.
logistics.bike_logistics: Transport bike via Swiss rail (SBB bike spaces) or fly with bike bag to ZRH; service courses likely at stage starts like pro event.
logistics.local_considerations: Expect road closures during pro race June 17-21; variable June weather with rain/heat; high altitude passes require acclimation.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic amateur multi-day road challenge shadowing the revamped Tour de Suisse pros.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Endurance road cyclists seeking pro-level Swiss Alps stages without WorldTour speed.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for stunning scenery and prestige of riding iconic routes.
pro_tips: Train for 20,000m+ elevation like past pro editions.
Sync with pro race for spectator vibes June 17-21.
Pack layers for alpine weather shifts.
